document.title: Usability Validation Report for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation report for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as usability validation evidence showing task completion results, participant feedback, residual usability risk conclusion, and referenced usability/risk management standards.
keyPoints: The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Seven participants were involved in the usability test.
Each usability task had a reported task completion success rate of 100% and pass result.
The report concludes that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.
The report references EN 60601-1-6:2010, EN 62366-1:2015, IEC TR 62366-2:2016, and EN ISO 14971:2019.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability validation report for the G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Report for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-10-107 (Rev.0).
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-10-107 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The document date is Nov 22, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Park, Jaehong Nov 22, 2024; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dongho Nov 22, 2024; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Nov 22, 2024

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was established on Nov 22, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Nov 22, 2024 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The product models covered are G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: This device (G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP) intended use is as follows:
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models

evidence.claim: The device is intended to obtain information necessary for patient diagnosis using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR, or DR.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Use

evidence.claim: The detector is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The report states that performance and usage are the same except for size, with no difference in usability.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Usability'
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models

evidence.claim: The usability test included seven participants.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants were involved in a usability test to ensure stable results.

evidence.claim: Participants were persons educated about medical devices and licensed equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.
evidence.evidenceText: Person who receives education about medical device and to have a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.

evidence.claim: The usability task completion success rate was 100% and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Task Completion Success Rate Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. Result: 100 % (Pass)

evidence.claim: The overall data summary reports ACT #1 through ACT #9 as pass with 100% task completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Task Completion ACT #1 ACT #2 ACT #3 ACT #4 ACT #5 ACT #6 ACT #7 ACT #8 ACT #9 Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass 100 % Pass

evidence.claim: The report says some problems were identified during usability testing and participant interviews and risk control measures are required.
evidence.evidenceText: Based the usability verification & evaluation report, some problems are carried out during the usability testing, session of participants interview. Proper risk control measures are required to implement into the design to reduce the derived risks.

evidence.claim: The report concludes that residual usability risk is minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable

evidence.claim: The report states no benefit-outweigh-risk evaluation is performed because the usability goal met the acceptance criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Since the usability verification & evaluation report demonstrates that the usability goal met the acceptance criteria set in the usability verification & evaluation plan, no evaluation of “benefit outweigh risk” is performed.

evidence.claim: The report states usability re-evaluation will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.
evidence.evidenceText: The re-evaluation of usability will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.

evidence.claim: The report references EN 60601-1-6:2010 for usability collateral requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability

evidence.claim: The report references EN 62366-1:2015 for application of usability engineering to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366-1:2015 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering to medical Devices

evidence.claim: The report references IEC TR 62366-2:2016 as guidance on applying usability engineering to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC TR 62366-2:2016 Medical devices — Part 2: Guidance on the application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2019 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text.
Manufacturer or legal entity name is not present in the source text.
Acceptance criteria wording repeatedly says 'over 100%' or 'over 100% of operators', which appears internally imprecise but is preserved from the source.

Further Improvement section states some problems were carried out during usability testing and risk control measures are required, while the Conclusion states residual usability risk is minimized and acceptable.
